title: lastrea tonkinensis
intro: Lastrea tonkinensis, also known as Tonkin fern or Tonkin lastrea, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Vietnam, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. In traditional herbal preparations, Lastrea tonkinensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to enhance overall well-being and promote a healthy lifestyle.
